original_description: Investigate Taskcluster task failures caused by worker images — extract image versions, compare passing vs failing tasks, find pass/fail cliffs across branches, debug Azure VMs via `az` CLI, and spin up throwaway debug VMs from the same image a pool uses. Use when a CI failure looks image-related. DO NOT USE FOR triggering a new image build (use worker-image-build).
best_description: Investigate Taskcluster task failures caused by worker images — extract image versions, compare passing vs failing tasks, find pass/fail cliffs across branches, debug Azure VMs via `az` CLI, and spin up throwaway debug VMs from the same image a pool uses. Use when a CI failure looks image-related. DO NOT USE FOR triggering a new image build (use worker-image-build).
final_description: ALWAYS USE WHEN the user mentions: SBOM (incl. UTF-16LE markdown artifacts), image version, image bump, "what changed in the image", pass/fail cliff, permafail after an image rollout, GenericWorker version, Windows build on a worker, `Get-HotFix`/drivers/filters on a worker VM, a `vm-...` worker name, `RG-TASKCLUSTER-WORKER-MANAGER-PRODUCTION`, or running PowerShell/`az vm run-command` against a Taskcluster worker. ALSO USE WHEN comparing two task IDs (passing vs failing) for image differences, identifying the date a `gecko-t/win*` or `b-win*` pool flipped green to red, or spinning up throwaway Azure debug VMs from a pool's image. The right skill any time a CI failure is suspected to be image-, SBOM-, or worker-VM-related — even when the user only gives a task ID and says "permafail" or "investigate". DO NOT USE FOR building a new image (use worker-image-build), plain task status/logs (use taskcluster), or Azure VM lifecycle/provisioning logs (use splunk or tc-logview).

history.description: Investigate Taskcluster task failures caused by worker images — extract image versions, compare passing vs failing tasks, find pass/fail cliffs across branches, debug Azure VMs via `az` CLI, and spin up throwaway debug VMs from the same image a pool uses. Use when a CI failure looks image-related. DO NOT USE FOR triggering a new image build (use worker-image-build).

history.description: Use for any investigation involving Taskcluster worker images, the Azure VMs running them, or task failures that might be image-related. Triggers: looking up image version / SBOM / Windows build / GenericWorker version for a worker pool or task; comparing two tasks (passing vs failing) for image differences; finding the date/cliff where an image bump caused regressions; running PowerShell or `az` commands against a worker VM (e.g. `vm-xyz` in `RG-TASKCLUSTER-WORKER-MANAGER-PRODUCTION`); checking `Get-HotFix`, hotfixes, drivers, or filesystem filters on a worker; spinning up a throwaway debug VM from a pool's image; decoding UTF-16LE SBOM artifacts. Use whenever a user names a `vm-...` worker, a `gecko-t/win*` or `b-win*` pool, an image version bump, or asks "what changed in the image". DO NOT USE FOR triggering a new image build (use worker-image-build).

history.description: Use this skill whenever a user investigates Taskcluster worker images or the Azure VMs running them. Triggers include: looking up image version, SBOM, Windows build, or GenericWorker version on a specific task, worker pool, or VM; comparing two task IDs (passing vs failing) to find image differences; identifying the date or cliff where an image bump caused regressions or a permafail; running PowerShell or `az vm run-command` against a worker VM (e.g. `vm-xxxxxx` in `RG-TASKCLUSTER-WORKER-MANAGER-PRODUCTION`); checking `Get-HotFix`, hotfixes, drivers, or filesystem filters on a worker; spinning up throwaway debug VMs from a pool's image; decoding UTF-16LE SBOM artifacts. Use whenever the user mentions a `vm-...` worker name, a `gecko-t/win*` or `b-win*` pool together with image/version/SBOM/Windows-build concerns, an image bump, or asks "what changed in the image". DO NOT trigger a new image build (use worker-image-build).

history.description: Investigate Firefox CI worker images and the Azure VMs running them. Use whenever the user wants to: extract image version, SBOM, Windows build, hotfixes, or GenericWorker version from a Taskcluster task, worker pool, or VM; compare two tasks (passing vs failing) to find image differences; identify the date or "cliff" where an image bump caused regressions or permafails; run PowerShell or `az vm run-command` against a worker VM; check `Get-HotFix`, drivers, or filesystem filters; spin up throwaway debug VMs from a pool's image; or decode UTF-16LE SBOM artifacts. Strong triggers: any `vm-...` worker name, any `gecko-t/win*` or `b-win*` pool with image/SBOM/Windows-build concerns, `RG-TASKCLUSTER-WORKER-MANAGER-PRODUCTION`, "image bump", "permafail after image", or "what changed in the image". Skip if the user wants to BUILD a new image (use worker-image-build), only fetch task status/logs (use taskcluster), or query Azure VM lifecycle/provisioning events (use splunk or tc-logview).
